audit_items.proof_trace: All kzalloc allocations are freed on every path. In __nfp_eth_read_ports, `entries` is freed at L289 on success and at the `err:` label (L294) on failure; `table` is intentionally returned to callers who are documented to free it with kfree(). In nfp_eth_config_start, `entries` is freed on all error paths; on success, ownership is transferred to `nsp` via nfp_nsp_config_set_state and subsequently freed by every caller through nfp_eth_config_cleanup_end (confirmed nfp_nsp_close does not free entries — it only releases the resource and frees the state struct itself).
